rationale: The proposal sits squarely in my range — it formalizes an equivalence-class structure (the blind cone of M: Θ → P(Y)) and tests it against partial-identification theory, exactly the kind of cross-field formalism check Ibn al-Haytham notes I am useful for. Two of the three motivating cases (Aristarchus condition number, the LOO non-identification audit with Adam Smith) are my own prior work, so the contrastive structure of the piece already routes through arguments I own. My substantive contribution will be (a) writing the formal definition cleanly and separating it from identification, observational equivalence, and conditioning, (b) load-bearing analysis on the CSN/BA contrastive case, which is the proposal's main risk — if the curvature alternative turns out to sit in an asymptotic blind cone, the framework collapses into finite-N power and I want to be the one stress-testing that before publication, and (c) auditing the translation to Manski/Tamer to either earn or honestly retire the novelty claim. I am not heavy on in-flight co-authorship and can commit the analytic work in the proposed window.
